seoTitle: Make a Deposit Without an Invoice in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record a deposit without an invoice in QuickBooks Online for cash sales, loan proceeds, or other income that doesn't start with a bill.
intro: Not every dollar coming into your business starts with an invoice — cash sales, loan proceeds, and miscellaneous payments all need a home in your books too. This guide shows how to make a deposit without an invoice in QuickBooks Online, so income is captured accurately without unnecessary paperwork. It's suited to small business owners and freelancers who deal with non-invoiced income regularly.

steps.heading: Open the deposit feature
steps.body: Navigate to the banking or deposits area of QuickBooks Online to start a new deposit entry.

steps.heading: Select the correct bank account
steps.body: Choose the account the money was actually deposited into so your bank reconciliation stays accurate.

steps.heading: Categorize the income source
steps.body: Assign the deposit to the appropriate income or other account, such as cash sales or loan proceeds, rather than leaving it uncategorized.

steps.heading: Enter the amount and details
steps.body: Fill in the deposit amount, date, and a brief memo describing the source of funds for future reference.

steps.heading: Reconcile with your bank statement
steps.body: Match the recorded deposit against your bank feed to confirm it lines up and avoid duplicate entries.

faq.question: When would I need to record a deposit without an invoice?
faq.answer: This applies to income like cash sales, loan proceeds, or refunds that don't originate from a billed invoice.

faq.question: Will this affect my sales reports?
faq.answer: It depends on how you categorize the deposit — assigning it to the right income account keeps your reports accurate.

faq.question: Can I connect this deposit to my bank feed automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once entered, you can match it against the corresponding transaction in your bank feed to reconcile accounts.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for freelancers?
faq.answer: Yes, freelancers who receive payments outside of formal invoices, like cash or personal transfers, can use this to keep records complete.
